author: Flippy
msg:
#amaquestion 

From actual real life data from past weeks we see that only ULOs with 95%+ consistent uptime are given max rewards. Every 10% lower will reduce rewards drastically and from 65% or lower it is near zero. Consisteny also seems to play an important role.

This is nearly impossible for everyday single phones. Especially in poorer countries.

Can you confirm there is a sort of tier system active?
Why are we not informed about these rules?

author: Calus B
msg: Why do people diversify? The thought is that if you’re properly diversified you’re not going to get wrecked if the one thing you invested in falls. If you’re in crypto and we had a bear market as we’ve all experienced pretty much everything gets wrecked no matter what so crypto alone is not diversification. It’s high risk. Theoretically, it’s higher reward. So you wanna pick a very small selection of projects do a ton of research on them and use that as your crypto exposure and then diversify into maybe more traditional market sources.

author: Calus B
msg: When you have only a small few projects, it’s easier to keep a very close eye on them and it’s easier to notice major problems if they pop up when you’re only tracking a few.

author: redman
msg:
you can do "{endpoint}/functions/v1/licenses_get_licenses 
with 
payload : {"role":"uno","licenseId":"0x03253....","skip":0,"take":1}
this gives you license details

and also

"{endpoint}/rest/v1/rpc/license_analytics_get_by_license" this gives you license stats

and 
"{endpoint}/rest/v1/rpc/rewards_get_allocations_summary?limit=1"

for license rewards

author: HFA | Earnest Nodes
msg:
Why am I not earning any rewards or why my rewards are so low?

Please note that rewards earnings are based on PoW (Proof of Work) which in simple terms means that the app must perform certain Telco tasks for the user to earn. Please also note that any of the situations below can cause a disconnection in the app hence paused earnings.

1. Phone is turned off. Unity app will be disconnected and no earnings will occur.
Solution: please keep your phone or device powered on at all times with no charge issues.

  2. App’s permission to work in the Background is turned off.
Solution: Please allow Unity app to work in the background of your device. ( Change your device settings)
 
  3. Unity app is acting up and not being responsive.
Solution: Please force-stop the app and try to reconnect to resolve the issue. ( Try powering off, or logging out and logging back in with the email address originally provided )

  4. Unbound License.
Your license may not be bound with your device hence causing pause in earnings.
Solution: Please select the 3 dots on the bottom right and then select “Work” from the menu bar, there, select the license to be bound and then hit continue. That should resolve the issue of app is being disconnected. ( You can also unbind and bind back in the license to fix the issue)

  5. No WiFi or Mobile Data connection.
Solution: Make sure that your device is connected to the WiFi network or mobile data connection at all times for the app to work in the background and earn rewards for you. No connection simply means no earnings or low earnings!

What does grey dot means on the left side of Unity license code?

The Grey dot ( The Green dot means that the app is connected to the internet and functional. How to find Dot in your app? Select the “key” icon on the bottom of the app to find the license number and the dot either green or grey on the left side) simply means that your Unity app is not connected to a WiFi or mobile data connection or any other reasons described above. That means that you are not  earning rewards. That is counter productive for both UNO( Unity Node Operator ie., Earnest Nodes) and ULOs( You, the Unity License Operator). 

IMPORTANT:
Please make sure that your device is on, connected to the internet and the app is working correctly without any issues. 

Please also see the image of the grey dot that is an indication of activity or inactivity in terms of earnings.

If you still are having an issue with the app, please contact us by email: 
You can also direct message us through the Unity app’s messaging feature ( Please make sure to turn it on by clicking on communication and selecting the message button).

Thank you
